name: Lying Lateral Neck Flexion (Side Neck Raise)
resistance_profile: Gravity loads cervical lateral flexion; torque peaks with the head near horizontal (mid-range) and eases at the top and bottom.
cues: Ear toward the shoulder in a pure side-bend
Very light load and slow tempo
Keep the movement in the neck, not the torso
execution_steps: Lie on your side on a bench with your head and neck hanging off the end.
Optionally hold a light plate against the top side of your head with a towel for padding.
Lower your head toward the floor into a gentle lateral stretch.
Side-bend your neck, raising your ear up toward your shoulder.
Pause at the top, then lower slowly under control.
Complete all reps, then switch sides.
good_when: You want to train the often-neglected lateral neck flexors in the frontal plane
You are a combat or contact-sport athlete needing neck strength in all directions
bad_when: You have any cervical pain or instability
You cannot control the head weight through a slow tempo
common_errors: Rotating or flexing the head instead of side-bending
Using too much load and losing control
progressions: Add a light plate held against the head
regressions: Perform bodyweight only with no plate
